latest_status.action_labels: To acknowledge receipt of and approve the teach-out plan and teach-out agreements.; To acknowledge receipt of the self-study report. To note the on-site evaluation visit by the Commission's representatives to the main campus at 1837 University Circle, P. O. Box 200, Cheyney, PA 19319 on February 26-March 1, 2023. To postpone a decision on the self-study report. To request a supplemental information report, due August 1, 2023, documenting (1) compliance with the institution's program responsibilities under existing federal title IV student financial aid programs, including but not limited to an update on the Federal Student Aid program review and recertification and the institution's Heightened Cash Monitoring (HCM2) (Standard II and VI); (2) financial planning and budget processes that are aligned with the mission, goals, and objectives and are evidence-based (Standard VI); and (3) an annual independent audit confirming financial viability with evidence of follow-up on any cited concerns, including but not limited to the audited financial statements for fiscal year 2022 (Standard VI and former Requirement of Affiliation 11). To require that the institution complete and submit for approval, by August 1, 2023, a contingency teach-out plan and teach-out agreements because the Secretary of Education has placed the institution on Heightened Cash Monitoring (HCM2) as required by the Commission's Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures and federal regulation 34 CFR 602.24(c)(2)(i). To direct a follow-up team visit following submission of the supplemental information report. To direct a prompt Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss the Commission's expectations. The next evaluation visit is scheduled for 2030-2031.; To acknowledge receipt of the show cause report. To note the visit by the Commission's representatives. To acknowledge receipt of the institution's Intent to Appear before the Commission to present its reasons why its accreditation should not be withdrawn. To note the presentation by the institution's representatives on November 21, 2019. To acknowledge receipt of the institution's request to present the following new information at the presentation, as approved by the Chair of the Commission: (1) Updated Response to the Report of the Team Visiting Cheyney University September 9-10, 2019, (2) accompanying appendices, and (3) a formal presentation for the appearance as approved by the Vice-Chair of the Commission. To reaffirm accreditation because the institution is now in compliance with Standard VI (Planning, Resources, and Institutional Improvement) and Requirement of Affiliation 11. To note that the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, Office of the Governor, has provided written assurance of the elimination of Cheyney University's debt to the Pennsylvania State System of Higher Education (PASSHE) and the Office of the Chancellor. To request a monitoring report, due September 1, 2020, demonstrating sustainability of implemented corrective measures and further evidence of (1) documented financial resources, funding base, and plans to assure long-term financial stability (Requirement of Affiliation 11); and (2) fiscal and human resources adequate to support its operations (Standard VI Planning, Resources and Institutional Improvement). In addition, to request a supplemental information report, due March 1, 2020, providing evidence of the resolution of Cheyney University's debt obligations to PASSHE and evidence of the payment plan negotiated with the United States Department of Education to satisfy the institution's title IV repayment obligations (Standard VI and Requirement of Affiliation 11). To direct a follow-up team visit following submission of the monitoring report. To direct a prompt Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss the Commission's expectations. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission about any and all significant developments relevant to this action. The next evaluation visit is scheduled for 2022-2023. To note that the Mid-Point Peer Review will be delayed.; To acknowledge receipt of the supplemental information report requested by the Commission action of June 22, 2023. To note the on-site follow-up team visit by the Commission's representatives to the main campus at 1837 University Circle, Cheyney, PA 19319 on September 5-6, 2023. To acknowledge receipt of additional information provided by the institution on October 23, 2023. To place the institution on probation and note that the institution's accreditation is in jeopardy because of insufficient evidence that the institution is currently in compliance with Standard II (Ethics and Integrity), Standard III (Design and Delivery of the Student Learning Experience), Standard VI (Planning, Resources, and Institutional Improvement), and former Requirements of Affiliation 5 and 11. To note the institution remains accredited while on probation. To note further that federal regulations limit the period during which an institution may be in non-compliance, which started on November 16, 2023. To request a monitoring report, due March 1, 2024, demonstrating evidence that the institution has achieved and can sustain ongoing compliance with Standard II (Ethics and Integrity); Standard III (Design and Delivery of the Student Learning Experience), Standard VI (Planning, Resources, and Institutional Improvement); and former Requirements of Affiliation 5 and 11, including but not limited to (1) compliance with all applicable government laws and regulations and Commission policies and procedures (Standard II, former Requirement of Affiliation 5, and Substantive Change Policy and Procedures); (2) compliance with federal Clery Act reporting (Standard II and former Requirement of Affiliation 5); (3) adequate and appropriate institutional review and approval on any student learning opportunities designed, delivered, or assessed by third-party providers (Standard III and Substantive Change Policy and Procedures); (4) periodic assessment of third-party providers, consideration of results, and follow-up when warranted (Standard III); (5) the assessment of the adequacy and efficient utilization of institutional resources required to support the institution's mission and goals (Standard VI); (6) financial planning and budget processes that are aligned with the mission, goals, and objectives and are evidence-based (Standard VI); (7) adequate fiscal and human resources, including physical and technical infrastructure, to support operations (Standard VI); (8) compliance with its program responsibilities under existing federal title IV and other state laws and regulations, including any audits of financial aid programs as required by federal and state regulations and updated financial information relating to the institution's Heightened Cash Monitoring (HCM2) status (Standard VI); and (9) an annual independent audit confirming financial viability with evidence of follow-up on any cited concerns, including but not limited to the audited financial statements for fiscal year 2023 (Standard VI and former Requirement of Affiliation 11) (Fourteenth Edition and Evidence Expectations by Standards Guidelines). To request that the monitoring report also include further evidence of (1) periodic assessment of the effectiveness of assessment processes utilized by the institution to improve educational effectiveness (Standard V); (2) an administration with members with credentials and professional experience consistent with the mission of the organization and their functional roles (Standard VII); and (3) systematic procedures for evaluating administrative units and for using assessment data to enhance operations (Standard VII). To require that the institution complete and submit for approval, by March 1, 2024, a comprehensive, implementable teach-out plan and signed teach-out agreements with appropriate teach-out partner institutions (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures). In accordance with Commission policy and federal regulations, the teach-out plan must provide for the equitable treatment of students to complete their education or transfer to another institution, if the Commission were to withdraw accreditation. To note the Commission may reject the teach-out plan and require resubmission if all of the required information is not provided. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission of its status with local, state, federal, and other relevant agencies. To direct a follow-up team visit following submission of the monitoring report. To direct a prompt Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss the Commission's expectations. The date of the next evaluation will be determined upon reaffirmation of accreditation.; To acknowledge the receipt of the contingency teach-out plan requested by the Commission action of June 22, 2023.

To note the on-site follow-up team visit by the Commission's representatives to the main campus at 1837 University Circle, Cheyney, PA 19319 on September 5-6, 2023. To acknowledge receipt of additional information provided by the institution on October 23, 2023. To place the institution on probation and note that the institution's accreditation is in jeopardy because of insufficient evidence that the institution is currently in compliance with Standard II (Ethics and Integrity), Standard III (Design and Delivery of the Student Learning Experience), Standard VI (Planning, Resources, and Institutional Improvement), and former Requirements of Affiliation 5 and 11. To note the institution remains accredited while on probation. To note further that federal regulations limit the period during which an institution may be in non-compliance, which started on November 16, 2023. To request a monitoring report, due March 1, 2024, demonstrating evidence that the institution has achieved and can sustain ongoing compliance with Standard II (Ethics and Integrity); Standard III (Design and Delivery of the Student Learning Experience), Standard VI (Planning, Resources, and Institutional Improvement); and former Requirements of Affiliation 5 and 11, including but not limited to (1) compliance with all applicable government laws and regulations and Commission policies and procedures (Standard II, former Requirement of Affiliation 5, and Substantive Change Policy and Procedures); (2) compliance with federal Clery Act reporting (Standard II and former Requirement of Affiliation 5); (3) adequate and appropriate institutional review and approval on any student learning opportunities designed, delivered, or assessed by third-party providers (Standard III and Substantive Change Policy and Procedures); (4) periodic assessment of third-party providers, consideration of results, and follow-up when warranted (Standard III); (5) the assessment of the adequacy and efficient utilization of institutional resources required to support the institution's mission and goals (Standard VI); (6) financial planning and budget processes that are aligned with the mission, goals, and objectives and are evidence-based (Standard VI); (7) adequate fiscal and human resources, including physical and technical infrastructure, to support operations (Standard VI); (8) compliance with its program responsibilities under existing federal title IV and other state laws and regulations, including any audits of financial aid programs as required by federal and state regulations and updated financial information relating to the institution's Heightened Cash Monitoring (HCM2) status (Standard VI); and (9) an annual independent audit confirming financial viability with evidence of follow-up on any cited concerns, including but not limited to the audited financial statements for fiscal year 2023 (Standard VI and former Requirement of Affiliation 11) (Fourteenth Edition and Evidence Expectations by Standards Guidelines). To request that the monitoring report also include further evidence of (1) periodic assessment of the effectiveness of assessment processes utilized by the institution to improve educational effectiveness (Standard V); (2) an administration with members with credentials and professional experience consistent with the mission of the organization and their functional roles (Standard VII); and (3) systematic procedures for evaluating administrative units and for using assessment data to enhance operations (Standard VII). To require that the institution complete and submit for approval, by March 1, 2024, a comprehensive, implementable teach-out plan and signed teach-out agreements with appropriate teach-out partner institutions (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures). In accordance with Commission policy and federal regulations, the teach-out plan must provide for the equitable treatment of students to complete their education or transfer to another institution, if the Commission were to withdraw accreditation. To note the Commission may reject the teach-out plan and require resubmission if all of the required information is not provided. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission of its status with local, state, federal, and other relevant agencies. To direct a follow-up team visit following submission of the monitoring report. To direct a prompt Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss the Commission's expectations. The date of the next evaluation will be determined upon reaffirmation of accreditation.

actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the self-study report. To note the on-site evaluation visit by the Commission's representatives to the main campus at 1837 University Circle, P. O. Box 200, Cheyney, PA 19319 on February 26-March 1, 2023. To postpone a decision on the self-study report. To request a supplemental information report, due August 1, 2023, documenting (1) compliance with the institution's program responsibilities under existing federal title IV student financial aid programs, including but not limited to an update on the Federal Student Aid program review and recertification and the institution's Heightened Cash Monitoring (HCM2) (Standard II and VI); (2) financial planning and budget processes that are aligned with the mission, goals, and objectives and are evidence-based (Standard VI); and (3) an annual independent audit confirming financial viability with evidence of follow-up on any cited concerns, including but not limited to the audited financial statements for fiscal year 2022 (Standard VI and former Requirement of Affiliation 11). To require that the institution complete and submit for approval, by August 1, 2023, a contingency teach-out plan and teach-out agreements because the Secretary of Education has placed the institution on Heightened Cash Monitoring (HCM2) as required by the Commission's Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures and federal regulation 34 CFR 602.24(c)(2)(i). To direct a follow-up team visit following submission of the supplemental information report. To direct a prompt Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss the Commission's expectations. The next evaluation visit is scheduled for 2030-2031.

actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the show cause report. To note the visit by the Commission's representatives. To acknowledge receipt of the institution's Intent to Appear before the Commission to present its reasons why its accreditation should not be withdrawn. To note the presentation by the institution's representatives on November 21, 2019. To acknowledge receipt of the institution's request to present the following new information at the presentation, as approved by the Chair of the Commission: (1) Updated Response to the Report of the Team Visiting Cheyney University September 9-10, 2019, (2) accompanying appendices, and (3) a formal presentation for the appearance as approved by the Vice-Chair of the Commission. To reaffirm accreditation because the institution is now in compliance with Standard VI (Planning, Resources, and Institutional Improvement) and Requirement of Affiliation 11. To note that the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, Office of the Governor, has provided written assurance of the elimination of Cheyney University's debt to the Pennsylvania State System of Higher Education (PASSHE) and the Office of the Chancellor. To request a monitoring report, due September 1, 2020, demonstrating sustainability of implemented corrective measures and further evidence of (1) documented financial resources, funding base, and plans to assure long-term financial stability (Requirement of Affiliation 11); and (2) fiscal and human resources adequate to support its operations (Standard VI Planning, Resources and Institutional Improvement). In addition, to request a supplemental information report, due March 1, 2020, providing evidence of the resolution of Cheyney University's debt obligations to PASSHE and evidence of the payment plan negotiated with the United States Department of Education to satisfy the institution's title IV repayment obligations (Standard VI and Requirement of Affiliation 11). To direct a follow-up team visit following submission of the monitoring report. To direct a prompt Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss the Commission's expectations. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission about any and all significant developments relevant to this action. The next evaluation visit is scheduled for 2022-2023. To note that the Mid-Point Peer Review will be delayed.

latest_status.action_labels: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, and ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation, effective June 30, 2024. To note the complex substantive change request includes the merger of Salus University with Drexel University, effective, June 30, 2024, the anticipated date of the transaction. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction, or immediate notification if there is any delay of the date of the closing of the transaction, by email to [email protected]. To note that Drexel University is the surviving institution. To note that the surviving institution is responsible for ensuring the submission of all required reports and information on behalf of the integrated institution. To note that Salus University will maintain accreditation for the sole purpose of implementing the teach-out plan and agreements and effectuating the change in ownership process with the United States Department of Education per the United States Department of Education's Updated Guidance and Procedures for Changes in Ownership, dated September 15, 2022 (Electronic Announcement ID: GENERAL-22-70). To note the action for Salus University on June 27, 2024. To further note that Salus University will cease accreditation effective June 30, 2025. To include the following additional location within the institution's scope of accreditation effective June 30, 2024: 8630 Old York Road, Elkins Park, PA 19027. To include the following other instructional sites within the institution's scope of accreditation: 7630 Germantown Ave, Philadelphia, PA 19118; 1200 West Godfrey Ave., Philadelphia, PA 19141; 8380 Old York Road, Suite 120, Elkins Park, PA 19027; and 8360 Old York Road, Suite 2100, Elkins Park, PA 19027. To require immediate notification in the institution portal when instruction commences at the additional location. To note that written documentation of review or approval from the following agency has been received: Pennsylvania Attorney General. To request a supplemental information report, due September 30, 2024, providing (1) updated status of the implementation of the complex substantive change (Substantive Change Policy and Complex Substantive Change Procedures); (2) written evidence of review, including but not limited to, approval of the transaction from all required licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including but not limited to: the United States Department of Education and the Pennsylvania Department of Education (Standard II); and (3) written evidence of the institution's updated Eligibility and Certification Approval Report (ECAR) reflecting the inclusion of the additional location. To direct a complex substantive change site visit to the main campus of Drexel University at 3141 Chestnut Street, Philadelphia, PA 19104 and to the additional location at 8630 Old York Road, Elkins Park, PA 19027 as soon as practicable but no later than six months after June 30, 2024, the anticipated date of the transaction. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note the Commission may rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision or the complex substantive change is not implemented within one year of this action. To direct the institution to conduct a new comprehensive evaluation in accordance with Commission policy and procedures and federal regulation 34 CFR § 602.22(h). To note that the institution will begin self-study in anticipation of an evaluation visit in 2026-2027.

latest_status.action_labels: Staff acted on behalf of the Commission to acknowledge receipt of the institution's complex substantive change request. To acknowledge receipt of the institution's request, dated March 8, 2024, to withdraw the complex substantive change request before the Commission has taken action. To withdraw the institution's request for a change in legal status, form of control, or ownership. To request a supplemental information report, due April 12, 2024, that provides additional information on the status of the institution (Substantive Change Policy and Procedures). To require that the institution complete and submit for approval by April 12, 2024, a comprehensive, implementable teach-out plan and agreements (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures). In accordance with Commission policy and federal regulations, the teach-out plan must provide for the equitable treatment of students to complete their education and include any signed teach-out agreements that the institution has entered into or intends to enter into with another institution. To note the evaluation visit has occurred and will be acted on by the Commission.; Staff acted on behalf of the Commission to acknowledge receipt of the institution's request dated September 6, 2024, to withdraw the substantive change request for institutional closure. To rescind the Commission action of April 25, 2024, directing that the teach-out plan be implemented because the institution has not closed. To note the Commission's action of April 25, 2024, requiring the institution to show cause why its accreditation should not be withdrawn.; Staff acted on behalf of the Commission to acknowledge receipt of the updated teach-out plan requested by the Commission action of November 21, 2024. To approve the teach-out agreements with: (1) Ceder Crest College, Allentown, PA; (2) East Stroudsburg University of Pennsylvania, East Stroudsburg, PA; (3) Frostburg State University, Frostburg, MD; (4) Lackawanna College, Scranton, PA; (5) Marywood University, Scranton, PA; (6) Misericordia University, Dallas, PA; (7) Moravian University; Bethlehem, PA; and (8) Wilkes University, Wilkes-Barre, PA. To postpone a decision on the following teach-out agreement because it did not include the required signatures: Chestnut Hill College, Philadelphia, PA. To remind the institution of its obligation to submit signed teach-out agreements as soon as they become available and to facilitate the transfer of students in a timely manner. To note that Misericordia University has been designated as the final repository of all Keystone College student records. To note that transfer of student records will not occur until appropriate based on the final disposition of the appeal. To note the Commission's action of December 20, 2024, and to note the institution retains its Accredited status until the completion of any appeal.; To acknowledge receipt of the institution's Notice of Intent to Appeal submitted on December 12, 2024. To note that the Administrator of the Appeal has determined that the Notice of Intent to Appeal was timely received, materially complete, and properly filed with the required deposit against expenses. To note the process for the appeal will be implemented in accordance with the Commission's Appeals from Adverse Actions Procedures. To note the institution retains its Accredited status until the completion of any appeal. To note the December 31, 2024, accreditation cease date is no longer in effect. The Commission will revise the effective date that accreditation will cease as appropriate based upon the final disposition of the appeal. To remind the institution of the Commission action of November 21, 2024, that directed the following conditions of the appeal: (1) the institution does not enroll new students; (2) the institution does not market to or recruit new students; (3) the institution maintains a clear and accurate statement about its accreditation phase and accreditation status for the public on its website in accordance with the Commission's Public Disclosures Policy and Procedures (Appeals from Adverse Actions Procedures); (4) the institution has an approved teach-out plan with signed teach-out agreements and is implementing it according to that approval (Teach-Out Plan and Agreements Policy and Procedures); and (5) the institution remains operational with students actively enrolled in its degree-granting programs, is not facing imminent closure, and does not close. To note that if the Commission determines that the institution has violated any of the conditions listed above or any other of the Commission policies and procedures, standards for accreditation, requirements of affiliation, or federal regulations, the Commission will take additional action (Appeals from Adverse Actions Procedures); the institution forfeits its appellate rights; and the Commission reserves the right to take a final adverse action which is no longer subject to appeal. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission of its status with local, state, and federal agencies and other relevant agencies, including programmatic accreditors. To note the updated teach-out plan and teach-out agreements, supplemental information report, and additional information recently requested and received on December 16, 2024, remains under review.; To acknowledge receipt of the institution's submission of a substantive change request for institutional closure. To note that the institution is in danger of imminent closure. To remind the institution of its obligation to plan for orderly closure. To note that the date accreditation will cease will be determined following the supplemental information report due May 15, 2024. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission immediately about any and all significant developments that may impact the institution's closure (Standard VII). To remind the institution of its requirement to show cause by August 1, 2024, requested by the Commission action of April 25, 2024.; To acknowledge receipt of the request by the institution on February 4, 2025, for the Commission to reconsider the adverse action to withdraw accreditation and to reconsider the conditions set during appeal. To note the Commission will honor the institution's request to reconsider the November 21, 2024, adverse action to withdraw the accreditation of Keystone College. To acknowledge receipt of new financial information, including the December 9, 2024, letter from the United States Department of Agriculture, which was received on February 10, 2025, and the complex substantive change request to enter into a transaction with the Washington Institute for Education and Research (WEIR), received February 21, 2025, neither of which was available at the time of the November 21, 2024, Commission action. To note the new financial information is significant and bears materially on the financial non-compliance identified by the Commission in the adverse action. To note this action renders the pending appeal of the adverse action to withdraw accreditation moot. To note that the Administrator of the Appeal is directed to take the steps necessary to terminate the pending appeal. To note that the conditions during appeal are no longer in effect. To remind the institution of its obligation to immediately inform the Commission regarding any changes in the status of the institution, including but not limited to obligations with the United States Department of Agriculture or the complex substantive change negotiations with WEIR. To remind the institution of its obligation to be transparent about its accreditation status with all constituents. To note that the Commission has placed the institution into the status of non-compliance show cause. To require the institution to continue to show cause, by September 2, 2025, to demonstrate why its accreditation should not be withdrawn because of insufficient evidence that the institution is in compliance with (1) all applicable government laws and regulations (Standard II); (2) documented financial resources, funding base, and plans for financial development adequate to support its educational purposes and programs and to ensure financial stability (Standard VI); (3) a record of responsible fiscal management, including preparing a multi-year budget (Standard VI); (4) program responsibilities under existing federal title IV and other state laws and regulations, including any audits of financial aid programs as required by federal and state regulations (Standard II and Standard VI); (5) a governing body that serves the public interest, ensures that the institution clearly states and fulfills its mission and goals, has fiduciary responsibility for the institution, and is ultimately accountable for the academic quality, integrity, planning, and fiscal well-being of the institution (Standard VII); and (6) a governing body that plays a basic policy-making role in financial affairs to ensure integrity and strong financial management, including but not limited to timely review of audited financial statements and other documents related to the fiscal viability of the institution (Standard VII). To note that the institution remains accredited while on show cause. To note further that federal regulations limit the period during which an institution may be in non-compliance, which started on April 25, 2024. To require a show cause report, due September 2, 2025, documenting evidence that the institution has achieved and can sustain ongoing compliance with the Commission's standards for accreditation, requirements of affiliation, policies and procedures, and applicable federal regulatory requirements. The show cause report must include evidence that the institution has: (1) all applicable government laws and regulations (Standard II); (2) documented financial resources, funding base, and plans for financial development, including those from any related entities, adequate to support its educational purposes and programs and to ensure financial stability (Standard VI); (3) a record of responsible fiscal management, including preparing a multi-year budget (Standard VI); and (4) complied with program responsibilities under existing federal title IV and other state laws and regulations, including through any audits of financial aid programs as required by federal and state regulations (Standard II and Standard VI); (5) a governing body that serves the public interest, ensures that the institution clearly states and fulfills its mission and goals, has fiduciary responsibility for the institution, and is ultimately accountable for the academic quality, integrity, planning, and fiscal well-being of the institution (Standard VII); and (6) a governing body that plays a basic policy-making role in financial affairs to ensure integrity and strong financial management, including but not limited to timely review of audited financial statements and other documents related to the fiscal viability of the institution (Standard VII). The show cause report must also include evidence of progress related to its pending transaction with the Washington Institution for Education and Research and compliance with the Commission's Complex Substantive Change review and procedures (Substantive Change Policy and Complex Substantive Change Procedures). To direct an on-site show cause visit following submission of the report. The purpose of the on-site show cause visit is to verify the information provided in the show cause report and the institution's ongoing and sustainable compliance with the Commission's standards for accreditation, requirements of affiliation, policies and procedures, and applicable federal regulatory requirements. To direct a prompt Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss the Commission's expectations. To note that the institution will be invited to present before the Commission when it meets to consider the institution's show cause report. To remind the institution of its obligation to make freely available to the Commission accurate, fair, and complete information on all aspects of the institution and its operations, including making additional information available to the Commission representatives when requested (Standard VII and Accreditation Review Cycle and Monitoring Policy and Procedures). The date of the next evaluation will be determined upon reaffirmation of accreditation. To note the Commission action of January 8, 2025, allowing the institution to submit a complex substantive change request. To allow the complex substantive change submission to proceed through the review process while on show cause. To remind the institution of its obligation to obtain approval to proceed with all other substantive changes while on non-compliance status in accordance with the Substantive Change Policy and Procedures.; To acknowledge receipt of the self-study report. To note the on-site evaluation visit by the Commission's representatives to the main campus at One College Green, La Plume, PA 18440 on March 17-20, 2024. To require the institution to show cause, by August 1, 2024, to demonstrate why its accreditation should not be withdrawn because of insufficient evidence that the institution is in compliance with Standard VI (Planning, Resources, and Institutional Improvement) and former Requirements of Affiliation 5, 6, and 11. To note that the institution remains accredited while on show cause. To note further that federal regulations limit the period during which an institution may be in non-compliance, which started on April 25, 2024. To require a show cause report, due August 1, 2024, documenting evidence that the institution has achieved and can sustain ongoing compliance with the Commission's standards for accreditation, requirements of affiliation, policies and procedures, and applicable federal regulatory requirements. The show cause report must include evidence that the institution has: (1) documented financial resources, funding base, and plans for financial development, including those from any related entities, adequate to support its educational purposes and programs and to ensure financial stability (Standard VI and former Requirement of Affiliation 11); (2) a record of responsible fiscal management, including preparing a multi-year budget (Standard VI and former Requirement of Affiliation 5); (3) an annual independent audit confirming financial viability and proper internal financial controls, with evidence of corrective measures taken to address any material findings cited in the audit or an accompanying management letter (Standard VI and former Requirement of Affiliation 5); and (4) complied with program responsibilities under existing federal title IV and other state laws and regulations, including through any audits of financial aid programs as required by federal and state regulations (Standard VI and former Requirement of Affiliation 6) (Fourteenth Edition and Evidence Expectations by Standard Guidelines). To direct an on-site show cause visit following submission of the report. The purpose of the on-site show cause visit is to verify the information provided in the show cause report and the institution's ongoing and sustainable compliance with the Commission's standards for accreditation, requirements of affiliation, policies and procedures, and applicable federal regulatory requirements. To require that the institution complete and submit for approval, by May 15, 2024, an updated, comprehensive, implementable teach-out plan and signed teach-out agreements with appropriate teach-out partner institutions, including any documentation supporting the agreements, as soon as they become available (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures). In accordance with Commission policy and federal regulations, the teach-out plan must provide for the equitable treatment of students to complete their education or transfer to another institution, if the Commission were to withdraw accreditation. To note the Commission may reject the teach-out plan and require resubmission if all of the required information is not provided. To require Keystone College to submit a supplemental information report, due May 15, 2024, providing evidence that the institution: (1) complies with all applicable government laws and regulations and Commission policies and procedures (Standard II); (2) represents itself honestly and truthfully in all publications and communications and with internal and external communities (Standard II); (3) has provided immediate notification of the status of the institution to the Keystone College community, including all governing board members, students, faculty, staff and others with significant roles (Standard II and Public Disclosures Policy and Procedures); and (4) has made freely available to the Commission accurate, fair, and complete information on all aspects of the institution and its operations, including any and all actions, votes, and resolutions taken by the governing board on April 5, 2024, or at any other time that impacts the status of the institution (Standard VII). To direct a prompt Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss the Commission's expectations. To note that the institution will be invited to present before the Commission when it meets to consider the institution's show cause report. To remind the institution of its obligation to make freely available to the Commission accurate, fair, and complete information on all aspects of the institution and its operations, including making additional information available to the Commission representatives when requested (Standard VII and Accreditation Review Cycle and Monitoring Policy and Procedures). The date of the next evaluation will be determined upon reaffirmation of accreditation.; To acknowledge receipt of the show cause report, submitted on August 1, 2024. To note the Commission actions of November 1, 2024. To note the on-site show cause visit by Commission representatives to the main campus at One College Green, La Plume, PA 18440 on August 27-28, 2024. To acknowledge receipt of the institution's intent to appear before the Commission to present its reasons why its accreditation should not be withdrawn. To note the presentation by the institution's representatives on November 20, 2024. To acknowledge receipt of the institution's request to present new information as approved by the Chair of the Commission: (1) letter of submission, (2) summary and evidence, and (3) a presentation for use during the show cause appearance. To withdraw accreditation from Keystone College effective December 31, 2024. The institution failed to (1) provide evidence that demonstrates compliance with Commission standards for accreditation (Standard II, Ethics and Integrity; Standard VI, Planning, Resources, and Institutional Improvement; and Standard VII, Governance, Leadership, and Administration), policies and procedures, and applicable federal regulatory requirements; (2) demonstrate the capacity to make required improvements; and (3) demonstrate that it can sustain itself in the short or long term (Accreditation Actions Policy and Procedures). The institution failed to provide evidence of compliance with (1) all applicable government laws and regulations (Standard II); (2) documented financial resources, funding base, and plans for financial development adequate to support its educational purposes and programs and to ensure financial stability (Standard VI); (3) a record of responsible fiscal management, including preparing a multi-year budget (Standard VI); (4) program responsibilities under existing federal title IV and other state laws and regulations, including any audits of financial aid programs as required by federal and state regulations (Standard II and Standard VI); (5) a governing body that serves the public interest, ensures that the institution clearly states and fulfills its mission and goals, has fiduciary responsibility for the institution, and is ultimately accountable for the academic quality, integrity, planning, and fiscal well-being of the institution (Standard VII); and (6) a governing body that plays a basic policy-making role in financial affairs to ensure integrity and strong financial management, including but not limited to timely review of audited financial statements and other documents related to the fiscal viability of the institution (Standard VII). This decision is based on the record before the Commission at the show cause appearance including: (1) a complex substantive change request submitted on September 1, 2023, for a change in legal status, form of control, or ownership and the institution's subsequent request on March 8, 2024, to withdraw the complex substantive change request, which was withdrawn by the Commission on April 4, 2024; (2) a self-study report submitted on February 1, 2024, the report of a subsequent visit by Commission representatives on March 17-20, 2024, and the institutional response; (3) a supplemental information report and teach-out plan and agreements submitted on April 12, 2024; (4) a substantive change request for institutional closure submitted on April 17, 2024, and the institution's subsequent request of September 6, 2024, to withdraw the request for institutional closure; (5) a supplemental information report and updated teach-out plan and agreements submitted on May 15, 2024; (6) a show cause report and teach-out plan and agreements submitted on August 1, 2024, the report of a subsequent visit by Commission representatives on August 27-28, 2024, and the institutional response; (7) information submitted by the institution on November 13, 2024, including new information submitted for the show cause appearance; and (8) a show cause appearance of institutional representatives on November 20, 2024. To approve the teach-out plan and teach-out agreements, requested by the Commission action of April 4, 2024, and to direct its implementation. To require an updated teach-out plan and teach-out agreements, due December 6, 2024, based on the adverse action to withdraw accreditation. To require a supplemental information report, due December 6, 2024, providing evidence of (1) executed teach-out agreements; (2) implementation of the teach-out plan; (3) completed and issued transcripts for any students wishing to transfer to another institution; (4) appropriate arrangements for the permanent disposition of student records so that students and alumni will be able to obtain accurate and complete transcripts in the future (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures); and (5) notifications and public disclosures. To direct the institution to immediately (1) notify all constituents of the withdrawal of accreditation, effective December 31, 2024; (2) notify the Keystone College community, including all governing board members, students, faculty, staff, and others with significant roles, of the status of the institution; and (3) provide accurate information regarding the institution's accreditation status with MSCHE on its website and wherever accreditation is referenced on the institution's web pages, publications, announcements, and in all other appropriate places and venues (Public Disclosures Policy and Procedures). To note this action is an adverse action subject to appeal in accordance with the Commission's Appeals from Adverse Actions Procedures. The institution retains its Accredited status until the completion of any appeal. An explanation of this type of action is provided in the Commission's Accreditation Actions Policy and Procedures. To note all final adverse actions are subject to the arbitration requirements in the Commission's Arbitration of Disputes Concerning Final Adverse Actions Procedures and federal regulation 34 CFR § 602.20(e). To note that the following conditions are in effect in the event of an appeal: (1) the institution does not enroll new students; (2) the institution does not market to or recruit new students; (3) the institution maintains a clear and accurate statement about its accreditation phase and accreditation status for the public on its website in accordance with the Commission's Public Disclosures Policy and Procedures (Appeals from Adverse Actions Procedures); (4) the institution has an approved teach-out plan with signed teach-out agreements and is implementing it according to that approval (Teach-Out Plan and Agreements Policy and Procedures); and (5) the institution remains operational with students actively enrolled in its degree-granting programs, is not facing imminent closure, and does not close. To note that if the Commission determines that the institution has violated any of the conditions listed above or any other of the Commission policies and procedures, standards for accreditation, requirements of affiliation, or federal regulations, the Commission will take additional action (Appeals from Adverse Actions Procedures); the institution forfeits its appellate rights; and the Commission reserves the right to take a final adverse action which is no longer subject to appeal. The Commission will revise the effective date that accreditation will cease as necessary. To note that the institution's request, dated September 6, 2024, to proceed with a complex substantive change while on non-compliance show cause (Complex Substantive Change Procedures Section III.D) is no longer under consideration by the Executive Committee due to the Commission's adverse action to withdraw accreditation.; To acknowledge receipt of the show cause report. To reaffirm accreditation because the institution is now in compliance with Standard II (Ethics and Integrity), Standard VI (Planning, Resources, and Institutional Improvement), and Standard VII (Governance, Leadership, and Administration). To note the on-site show cause visit by Commission representatives to the main campus at One College Green, La Plume, PA 18440 on November 5-7, 2025. To note that the requirement to appear before the Commission to show cause why its accreditation should not be withdrawn is no longer required. To request a monitoring report, due September 1, 2026, demonstrating sustainability of implemented corrective measures and further evidence of (1) all applicable government laws and regulations (Standard II); (2) documented financial resources, funding base, and plans for financial development adequate to support its educational purposes and programs and to ensure financial stability (Standard VI); (3) a record of responsible fiscal management, including preparing a multi-year budget (Standard VI); (4) program responsibilities under existing federal Title IV and other state laws and regulations, including any audits of financial aid programs as required by federal and state regulations (Standard II and Standard VI); (5) a governing body that serves the public interest, ensures that the institution clearly states and fulfills its mission and goals, has fiduciary responsibility for the institution, and is ultimately accountable for the academic quality, integrity, planning, and fiscal well-being of the institution (Standard VII); and (6) a governing body that plays a basic policy-making role in financial affairs to ensure integrity and strong financial management, including but not limited to timely review of audited financial statements and other documents related to the fiscal viability of the institution (Standard VII). To request that the monitoring report also address: (1) the updated status of the implementation of the complex substantive change (Substantive Change Policy and Complex Substantive Change Procedures); (2) compliance with all applicable government laws and regulations, specifically the final resolution and response from the United States Department of Education and the U.S. Attorney's Office (Standard II); (3) the implementation of a formal process to periodically assess ethics and integrity as evidenced in institutional policies, processes, and practices (Standard II); (4) a record of responsible fiscal management with evidence of corrective measures taken to address any findings cited in the fiscal year 2024 and fiscal year 2025 audited financial statements or accompanying management letters (Standard VI); and (5) a clearly articulated governance structure that includes a documented process for the regular evaluation of the president and a formal process of board self-assessment for continuous improvement (Standard VII) (Fourteenth Edition and Evidence Expectations by Standard Guidelines). To direct a follow-up team visit following submission of the monitoring report. To direct a prompt Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss Commission expectations.; To acknowledge receipt of the teach-out plan and teach-out agreements, requested by the Commission action of April 4, 2024. To note that the institution is in danger of imminent closure. To remind the institution of its obligation to plan for orderly closure. To direct the institution to implement the teach-out plan. To require that the institution complete and submit for approval, by May 15, 2024, an updated, comprehensive, and implementable teach-out plan to include: (1) signed and executed teach-out agreements with appropriate teach-out partner institutions, including any documentation supporting the agreements, as soon as they become available; (2) evidence that required notifications regarding closure to all relevant stakeholders have taken place; (3) evidence of arrangements for the permanent disposition of student records so that students and alumni will be able to obtain accurate and complete transcripts, now and in the future; (4) evidence of the production and issuance of transcripts for all students; (5) evidence that communications regarding student records has occurred; and (6) evidence that financial aid counseling has been conducted, including students' rights to closed school loan discharges (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures). In accordance with Commission policy and federal regulations, the teach-out plan must provide for the equitable treatment of students to complete their education or transfer to another institution. To note the Commission may reject the teach-out plan and require resubmission if all of the required information is not provided. To remind the institution of its requirement to show cause by August 1, 2024, requested by the Commission action of April 25, 2024.; To acknowledge receipt of the updated teach-out plan required by the Commission action of February 27, 2025. To note the Commission action of November 21, 2024, to approve the teach-out plan. To note the Commission directive to implement the teach-out plan is no longer required. To remind the institution of its obligation to submit new signed teach-out agreements as soon as they become available (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures).; To approve the teach-out plan and teach-out agreements, requested by the Commission action of April 4, 2024, and to direct its implementation. To require an updated teach-out plan and teach-out agreements, due December 6, 2024, based on the adverse action to withdraw accreditation.; To note the Commission actions of November 21, 2024, to approve the teach-out plan and the Commission action of January 9, 2025, to acknowledge receipt of the updated teach-out plan. To note the Commission directive to implement the teach-out plan is no longer required. To require an updated teach-out plan with any new, not yet approved teach-out agreements, due April 18, 2025, based on the non-compliance show cause status. To approve the teach-out agreement with Chestnut Hill College, Philadelphia, PA. To note the teach-out agreements previously approved remain in effect.; To note the institution received the notification of adverse action of November 21, 2024, and a hardcopy of the action notification with delivery confirmation on November 26, 2024. To acknowledge receipt of the Acknowledgement of Adverse Action and Option to Exercise the Right to an Appeal on November 26, 2024. To note the acknowledgement was timely received, and that the institution must submit the Notice of Intent to Appeal by December 12, 2024, in accordance with the Commission's Appeals from Adverse Actions Procedures.

To note that the institution will be invited to present before the Commission when it meets to consider the institution's show cause report. To remind the institution of its obligation to make freely available to the Commission accurate, fair, and complete information on all aspects of the institution and its operations, including making additional information available to the Commission representatives when requested (Standard VII and Accreditation Review Cycle and Monitoring Policy and Procedures). The date of the next evaluation will be determined upon reaffirmation of accreditation. To note the Commission action of January 8, 2025, allowing the institution to submit a complex substantive change request. To allow the complex substantive change submission to proceed through the review process while on show cause. To remind the institution of its obligation to obtain approval to proceed with all other substantive changes while on non-compliance status in accordance with the Substantive Change Policy and Procedures.
actions.action_label_short: Continued Show Cause
actions.notes: To acknowledge receipt of the request by the institution on February 4, 2025, for the Commission to reconsider the adverse action to withdraw accreditation and to reconsider the conditions set during appeal. To note the Commission will honor the institution's request to reconsider the November 21, 2024, adverse action to withdraw the accreditation of Keystone College. To acknowledge receipt of new financial information, including the December 9, 2024, letter from the United States Department of Agriculture, which was received on February 10, 2025, and the complex substantive change request to enter into a transaction with the Washington Institute for Education and Research (WEIR), received February 21, 2025, neither of which was available at the time of the November 21, 2024, Commission action. To note the new financial information is significant and bears materially on the financial non-compliance identified by the Commission in the adverse action. To note this action renders the pending appeal of the adverse action to withdraw accreditation moot. To note that the Administrator of the Appeal is directed to take the steps necessary to terminate the pending appeal. To note that the conditions during appeal are no longer in effect. To remind the institution of its obligation to immediately inform the Commission regarding any changes in the status of the institution, including but not limited to obligations with the United States Department of Agriculture or the complex substantive change negotiations with WEIR. To remind the institution of its obligation to be transparent about its accreditation status with all constituents. To note that the Commission has placed the institution into the status of non-compliance show cause. To require the institution to continue to show cause, by September 2, 2025, to demonstrate why its accreditation should not be withdrawn because of insufficient evidence that the institution is in compliance with (1) all applicable government laws and regulations (Standard II); (2) documented financial resources, funding base, and plans for financial development adequate to support its educational purposes and programs and to ensure financial stability (Standard VI); (3) a record of responsible fiscal management, including preparing a multi-year budget (Standard VI); (4) program responsibilities under existing federal title IV and other state laws and regulations, including any audits of financial aid programs as required by federal and state regulations (Standard II and Standard VI); (5) a governing body that serves the public interest, ensures that the institution clearly states and fulfills its mission and goals, has fiduciary responsibility for the institution, and is ultimately accountable for the academic quality, integrity, planning, and fiscal well-being of the institution (Standard VII); and (6) a governing body that plays a basic policy-making role in financial affairs to ensure integrity and strong financial management, including but not limited to timely review of audited financial statements and other documents related to the fiscal viability of the institution (Standard VII). To note that the institution remains accredited while on show cause. To note further that federal regulations limit the period during which an institution may be in non-compliance, which started on April 25, 2024. To require a show cause report, due September 2, 2025, documenting evidence that the institution has achieved and can sustain ongoing compliance with the Commission's standards for accreditation, requirements of affiliation, policies and procedures, and applicable federal regulatory requirements. The show cause report must include evidence that the institution has: (1) all applicable government laws and regulations (Standard II); (2) documented financial resources, funding base, and plans for financial development, including those from any related entities, adequate to support its educational purposes and programs and to ensure financial stability (Standard VI); (3) a record of responsible fiscal management, including preparing a multi-year budget (Standard VI); and (4) complied with program responsibilities under existing federal title IV and other state laws and regulations, including through any audits of financial aid programs as required by federal and state regulations (Standard II and Standard VI); (5) a governing body that serves the public interest, ensures that the institution clearly states and fulfills its mission and goals, has fiduciary responsibility for the institution, and is ultimately accountable for the academic quality, integrity, planning, and fiscal well-being of the institution (Standard VII); and (6) a governing body that plays a basic policy-making role in financial affairs to ensure integrity and strong financial management, including but not limited to timely review of audited financial statements and other documents related to the fiscal viability of the institution (Standard VII). The show cause report must also include evidence of progress related to its pending transaction with the Washington Institution for Education and Research and compliance with the Commission's Complex Substantive Change review and procedures (Substantive Change Policy and Complex Substantive Change Procedures). To direct an on-site show cause visit following submission of the report. The purpose of the on-site show cause visit is to verify the information provided in the show cause report and the institution's ongoing and sustainable compliance with the Commission's standards for accreditation, requirements of affiliation, policies and procedures, and applicable federal regulatory requirements. To direct a prompt Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss the Commission's expectations. To note that the institution will be invited to present before the Commission when it meets to consider the institution's show cause report. To remind the institution of its obligation to make freely available to the Commission accurate, fair, and complete information on all aspects of the institution and its operations, including making additional information available to the Commission representatives when requested (Standard VII and Accreditation Review Cycle and Monitoring Policy and Procedures). The date of the next evaluation will be determined upon reaffirmation of accreditation. To note the Commission action of January 8, 2025, allowing the institution to submit a complex substantive change request. To allow the complex substantive change submission to proceed through the review process while on show cause. To remind the institution of its obligation to obtain approval to proceed with all other substantive changes while on non-compliance status in accordance with the Substantive Change Policy and Procedures.

actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the institution's Notice of Intent to Appeal submitted on December 12, 2024. To note that the Administrator of the Appeal has determined that the Notice of Intent to Appeal was timely received, materially complete, and properly filed with the required deposit against expenses. To note the process for the appeal will be implemented in accordance with the Commission's Appeals from Adverse Actions Procedures. To note the institution retains its Accredited status until the completion of any appeal. To note the December 31, 2024, accreditation cease date is no longer in effect. The Commission will revise the effective date that accreditation will cease as appropriate based upon the final disposition of the appeal. To remind the institution of the Commission action of November 21, 2024, that directed the following conditions of the appeal: (1) the institution does not enroll new students; (2) the institution does not market to or recruit new students; (3) the institution maintains a clear and accurate statement about its accreditation phase and accreditation status for the public on its website in accordance with the Commission's Public Disclosures Policy and Procedures (Appeals from Adverse Actions Procedures); (4) the institution has an approved teach-out plan with signed teach-out agreements and is implementing it according to that approval (Teach-Out Plan and Agreements Policy and Procedures); and (5) the institution remains operational with students actively enrolled in its degree-granting programs, is not facing imminent closure, and does not close. To note that if the Commission determines that the institution has violated any of the conditions listed above or any other of the Commission policies and procedures, standards for accreditation, requirements of affiliation, or federal regulations, the Commission will take additional action (Appeals from Adverse Actions Procedures); the institution forfeits its appellate rights; and the Commission reserves the right to take a final adverse action which is no longer subject to appeal. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission of its status with local, state, and federal agencies and other relevant agencies, including programmatic accreditors. To note the updated teach-out plan and teach-out agreements, supplemental information report, and additional information recently requested and received on December 16, 2024, remains under review.

actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the show cause report, submitted on August 1, 2024. To note the Commission actions of November 1, 2024. To note the on-site show cause visit by Commission representatives to the main campus at One College Green, La Plume, PA 18440 on August 27-28, 2024. To acknowledge receipt of the institution's intent to appear before the Commission to present its reasons why its accreditation should not be withdrawn. To note the presentation by the institution's representatives on November 20, 2024. To acknowledge receipt of the institution's request to present new information as approved by the Chair of the Commission: (1) letter of submission, (2) summary and evidence, and (3) a presentation for use during the show cause appearance. To withdraw accreditation from Keystone College effective December 31, 2024. The institution failed to (1) provide evidence that demonstrates compliance with Commission standards for accreditation (Standard II, Ethics and Integrity; Standard VI, Planning, Resources, and Institutional Improvement; and Standard VII, Governance, Leadership, and Administration), policies and procedures, and applicable federal regulatory requirements; (2) demonstrate the capacity to make required improvements; and (3) demonstrate that it can sustain itself in the short or long term (Accreditation Actions Policy and Procedures). The institution failed to provide evidence of compliance with (1) all applicable government laws and regulations (Standard II); (2) documented financial resources, funding base, and plans for financial development adequate to support its educational purposes and programs and to ensure financial stability (Standard VI); (3) a record of responsible fiscal management, including preparing a multi-year budget (Standard VI); (4) program responsibilities under existing federal title IV and other state laws and regulations, including any audits of financial aid programs as required by federal and state regulations (Standard II and Standard VI); (5) a governing body that serves the public interest, ensures that the institution clearly states and fulfills its mission and goals, has fiduciary responsibility for the institution, and is ultimately accountable for the academic quality, integrity, planning, and fiscal well-being of the institution (Standard VII); and (6) a governing body that plays a basic policy-making role in financial affairs to ensure integrity and strong financial management, including but not limited to timely review of audited financial statements and other documents related to the fiscal viability of the institution (Standard VII). This decision is based on the record before the Commission at the show cause appearance including: (1) a complex substantive change request submitted on September 1, 2023, for a change in legal status, form of control, or ownership and the institution's subsequent request on March 8, 2024, to withdraw the complex substantive change request, which was withdrawn by the Commission on April 4, 2024; (2) a self-study report submitted on February 1, 2024, the report of a subsequent visit by Commission representatives on March 17-20, 2024, and the institutional response; (3) a supplemental information report and teach-out plan and agreements submitted on April 12, 2024; (4) a substantive change request for institutional closure submitted on April 17, 2024, and the institution's subsequent request of September 6, 2024, to withdraw the request for institutional closure; (5) a supplemental information report and updated teach-out plan and agreements submitted on May 15, 2024; (6) a show cause report and teach-out plan and agreements submitted on August 1, 2024, the report of a subsequent visit by Commission representatives on August 27-28, 2024, and the institutional response; (7) information submitted by the institution on November 13, 2024, including new information submitted for the show cause appearance; and (8) a show cause appearance of institutional representatives on November 20, 2024. To approve the teach-out plan and teach-out agreements, requested by the Commission action of April 4, 2024, and to direct its implementation. To require an updated teach-out plan and teach-out agreements, due December 6, 2024, based on the adverse action to withdraw accreditation. To require a supplemental information report, due December 6, 2024, providing evidence of (1) executed teach-out agreements; (2) implementation of the teach-out plan; (3) completed and issued transcripts for any students wishing to transfer to another institution; (4) appropriate arrangements for the permanent disposition of student records so that students and alumni will be able to obtain accurate and complete transcripts in the future (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures); and (5) notifications and public disclosures. To direct the institution to immediately (1) notify all constituents of the withdrawal of accreditation, effective December 31, 2024; (2) notify the Keystone College community, including all governing board members, students, faculty, staff, and others with significant roles, of the status of the institution; and (3) provide accurate information regarding the institution's accreditation status with MSCHE on its website and wherever accreditation is referenced on the institution's web pages, publications, announcements, and in all other appropriate places and venues (Public Disclosures Policy and Procedures). To note this action is an adverse action subject to appeal in accordance with the Commission's Appeals from Adverse Actions Procedures. The institution retains its Accredited status until the completion of any appeal. An explanation of this type of action is provided in the Commission's Accreditation Actions Policy and Procedures. To note all final adverse actions are subject to the arbitration requirements in the Commission's Arbitration of Disputes Concerning Final Adverse Actions Procedures and federal regulation 34 CFR § 602.20(e). To note that the following conditions are in effect in the event of an appeal: (1) the institution does not enroll new students; (2) the institution does not market to or recruit new students; (3) the institution maintains a clear and accurate statement about its accreditation phase and accreditation status for the public on its website in accordance with the Commission's Public Disclosures Policy and Procedures (Appeals from Adverse Actions Procedures); (4) the institution has an approved teach-out plan with signed teach-out agreements and is implementing it according to that approval (Teach-Out Plan and Agreements Policy and Procedures); and (5) the institution remains operational with students actively enrolled in its degree-granting programs, is not facing imminent closure, and does not close. To note that if the Commission determines that the institution has violated any of the conditions listed above or any other of the Commission policies and procedures, standards for accreditation, requirements of affiliation, or federal regulations, the Commission will take additional action (Appeals from Adverse Actions Procedures); the institution forfeits its appellate rights; and the Commission reserves the right to take a final adverse action which is no longer subject to appeal. The Commission will revise the effective date that accreditation will cease as necessary. To note that the institution's request, dated September 6, 2024, to proceed with a complex substantive change while on non-compliance show cause (Complex Substantive Change Procedures Section III.D) is no longer under consideration by the Executive Committee due to the Commission's adverse action to withdraw accreditation.

actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the teach-out plan and teach-out agreements, requested by the Commission action of April 4, 2024. To note that the institution is in danger of imminent closure. To remind the institution of its obligation to plan for orderly closure. To direct the institution to implement the teach-out plan. To require that the institution complete and submit for approval, by May 15, 2024, an updated, comprehensive, and implementable teach-out plan to include: (1) signed and executed teach-out agreements with appropriate teach-out partner institutions, including any documentation supporting the agreements, as soon as they become available; (2) evidence that required notifications regarding closure to all relevant stakeholders have taken place; (3) evidence of arrangements for the permanent disposition of student records so that students and alumni will be able to obtain accurate and complete transcripts, now and in the future; (4) evidence of the production and issuance of transcripts for all students; (5) evidence that communications regarding student records has occurred; and (6) evidence that financial aid counseling has been conducted, including students' rights to closed school loan discharges (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures). In accordance with Commission policy and federal regulations, the teach-out plan must provide for the equitable treatment of students to complete their education or transfer to another institution. To note the Commission may reject the teach-out plan and require resubmission if all of the required information is not provided. To remind the institution of its requirement to show cause by August 1, 2024, requested by the Commission action of April 25, 2024.
actions.action_label_short: To note that the institution is in danger of imminent closure.

To require that the institution complete and submit for approval, by May 15, 2024, an updated, comprehensive, and implementable teach-out plan to include: (1) signed and executed teach-out agreements with appropriate teach-out partner institutions, including any documentation supporting the agreements, as soon as they become available; (2) evidence that required notifications regarding closure to all relevant stakeholders have taken place; (3) evidence of arrangements for the permanent disposition of student records so that students and alumni will be able to obtain accurate and complete transcripts, now and in the future; (4) evidence of the production and issuance of transcripts for all students; (5) evidence that communications regarding student records has occurred; and (6) evidence that financial aid counseling has been conducted, including students' rights to closed school loan discharges (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures). In accordance with Commission policy and federal regulations, the teach-out plan must provide for the equitable treatment of students to complete their education or transfer to another institution. To note the Commission may reject the teach-out plan and require resubmission if all of the required information is not provided. To remind the institution of its requirement to show cause by August 1, 2024, requested by the Commission action of April 25, 2024.
actions.source_title: MSCHE Statement of Accreditation Status - Keystone College

To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission immediately about any and all significant developments that may impact the institution's closure (Standard VII). To remind the institution of its requirement to show cause by August 1, 2024, requested by the Commission action of April 25, 2024.
